title: Hotel in Rapid City, SD
location: La Quinta Inn and Suites by Wyndham at WaTiki Waterpark
address: 1416 N Elk Vale Rd, Rapid City, SD 57703

title: Drive - Rapid City to Devils Tower
Drive - Devils Tower to east Yellowstone entrance
Yellowstone River Picnic Area
description: 🦬 Wildlife: A great first stop just inside the East Entrance — the Yellowstone River corridor here is an excellent wildlife viewing area. Scan the hillsides and riverbanks for bison, elk, and raptors.

title: Trout Lake Trail & Fishing
description: 🥾 Short Hike & Lure Fishing: 1.2 mi loop, easy. A peaceful alpine lake hike with frequent river otter and osprey sightings. Trout Lake is a premier spot for spin-fishing with lead-free, barbless lures. Target large native cutthroat trout (must release unharmed) and rainbow trout from the shore. NOTE: The inlet stream and cove are permanently closed to fishing to protect spawning trout.

title: Lamar Valley Wildlife Viewing
description: 🦬 Wildlife: The crown jewel of Yellowstone wildlife viewing. Bison herds, wolves, grizzly bears, and pronghorn are all commonly spotted. Pull off at any turnout and scan the hillsides with binoculars — early afternoon is still productive but dawn/dusk are peak times.

title: Tower Fall
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: A dramatic 132-ft waterfall carved through volcanic rock. Short walk to the overlook. The basalt columns framing the falls are a great example of the park's volcanic geology.

title: Calcite Springs Overlook & Petrified Tree
description: 🌋 Geologic Features: Calcite Springs Overlook offers dramatic views of columnar basalt cliffs dropping into the Yellowstone River canyon. Just up the road, the Petrified Tree is a lone ancient redwood turned to stone by volcanic ash millions of years ago — a quick roadside stop.

title: Wraith Falls
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 0.8 mi out-and-back, easy. A beautiful 100-ft cascade tucked into a meadow near Mammoth. One of the most rewarding short hikes in the northern range — easy enough for the whole family.

title: Hotel in Gardiner, MT
location: Travelodge by Wyndham Gardiner Yellowstone Park North

title: Mammoth Hot Springs Terraces
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: A surreal landscape of travertine terraces built by calcium carbonate-rich water. Walk the Upper Terrace Loop (1.5 mi, easy boardwalk) to explore both active steaming terraces and pale dormant formations. One of the most otherworldly sights in the park.

title: Norris Geyser Basin
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: The hottest and most dynamic thermal basin in the park. Steamboat Geyser is the world's tallest active geyser. Walk the Back Basin and Porcelain Basin loops (combined ~2.5 mi, easy boardwalks) to see boiling pools, fumaroles, and Echinus Geyser.

title: Artist Paint Pots Trail
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 0.9 mi loop, easy. A fun, accessible trail winding through colorful mud pots and small geysers. The bubbling pink and gray mud pots are a highlight — great for kids.

title: Grand Prismatic Spring & Fairy Falls
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature + 🥾 Hike: Grand Prismatic Spring is Yellowstone's largest hot spring and most photographed thermal feature. Start with the Fairy Falls Trail (5.4 mi out-and-back, easy) — the overlook at mile 1 gives the iconic aerial view of the rainbow-colored spring. Continue to Fairy Falls itself, a stunning 200-ft waterfall at the end of the trail.

title: Old Faithful & Upper Geyser Basin
description: ⛲ Geysers: Catch an Old Faithful eruption (roughly every 90 min — check the predicted time at the visitor center). Then walk the Upper Geyser Basin boardwalk, which has the highest concentration of geysers on Earth. Castle, Grand, and Riverside geysers are all within a short walk of Old Faithful.

title: Hayden Valley Wildlife Viewing
description: 🦬 Wildlife: Prime bison and grizzly bear territory on the drive toward West Yellowstone. Slow down and scan the open meadows — this broad glacially-carved valley is one of the best places in the park to spot large mammals. Pelican Valley and the Yellowstone Lake shoreline nearby are excellent for waterfowl and trumpeter swans.

title: West Gate KOA
location: Yellowstone Park / West Gate KOA Holiday
address: 3305 Targhee Pass Highway 6 miles West on, US-20, West Yellowstone, MT 59758

title: West Thumb Geyser Basin
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: A unique spot where hydrothermal features meet the shore of Yellowstone Lake. Walk the short boardwalk loop past boiling pools and the famous Fishing Cone — a submerged geyser where early visitors would cook fish directly in the boiling water.

title: Storm Point Trail
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 2.3 mi loop, easy. Hugs the Yellowstone Lake shoreline with frequent bison and waterfowl sightings. Check the trailhead for bear activity before heading out — this area sees regular grizzly use. Great views across the lake toward the Absaroka Range.

title: Natural Bridge Trail
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 2 mi out-and-back, easy. Leads to a 51-ft rhyolite arch carved by Bridge Creek — one of the park's lesser-known geological gems. A quiet trail away from the crowds near Bridge Bay.

title: Lone Star Geyser Trail
description: 🥾 Hike + ⛲ Geyser: 4.8 mi out-and-back, easy. Follows the Firehole River through a peaceful backcountry corridor to a cone geyser that erupts every ~3 hours with a 30–50 ft column. Check the logbook at the trailhead for the last eruption time to time your arrival. One of the most rewarding uncrowded experiences in the park.

title: Yellowstone Lake & Grant Village Wildlife
description: 🦬 Wildlife: The meadows near Grant Village often have bison and elk in the early morning and evening. Yellowstone Lake is a hotspot for osprey, bald eagles, and river otters along the shoreline. Keep an eye out for black bears along the forest edges on the south entrance road as you head toward Jackson.

title: Spin Fishing at Lewis Lake
description: 🎣 Lure Fishing: Lewis Lake is renowned for excellent shore lure fishing. Cast lead-free, barbless spinners (like Panther Martins) or spoons (like Jake's Spin-A-Lure) from the shore near the lake inlet/outlet or pullouts along the South Entrance Road. Good chance of catching Lake Trout or Brown Trout.

title: Jackson Lake Lodge
address: 101 Jackson Lake Lodge Rd, Moran, WY 83013
